creative_brief: Split-screen hero: left side shows a grey, fluorescent-lit supermarket meat aisle with shrink-wrapped trays and distant use-by dates; right side shows a sunlit farm gate with cattle on green pasture and a FarmThru-branded crate being loaded. Earthy palette — deep greens, warm browns, clean white typography in sentence case. Below the fold, a simple illustrated supply chain diagram contrasting the six-step supermarket chain versus FarmThru's three-step path.
headline: Your supermarket beef travelled 2,400km. Ours travelled from the farm yesterday.
subhead: FarmThru is a farm-direct regenerative grocery store. No warehouses, no wholesalers — just NSW farms and a Brookvale hub where you collect food that's days old, not weeks old.
hero_copy:
You didn't plan to switch. Nobody does. But then you noticed the use-by date was three days away on chicken you just bought. You wondered how long it had actually been sitting in cold storage. You Googled it. And you couldn't un-Google it.

Supermarket beef spends 14–21 days in cold storage after travelling thousands of kilometres through warehouses and wholesalers. Ours comes from regenerative NSW farms straight to our Brookvale hub. Days old, not weeks. That's not marketing — that's a shorter supply chain.

sections.heading: What you're actually comparing
sections.body:
This isn't about organic labels or fancy branding. It's about two completely different supply chains.

The big chains: farm → processor → warehouse → wholesaler → distribution centre → store shelf. Every stop adds days, skims margin from the farmer, and moves your food further from fresh.

FarmThru: farm → hub → you. That's it. Zero warehouses. Zero wholesalers. Zero middlemen.

We work with around 15–20 partner farms and suppliers across NSW. 100% grass-fed beef. Pasture-raised chicken. Wild-caught salmon. Heritage pork. Eggs from birds that actually see daylight. No hormones, no antibiotics, no feedlot finishing.

You order online when you want — no subscription, no lock-in, no membership fees. Collect from our Brookvale hub Monday to Friday. Bulk buying keeps prices fair for families who commit to eating this way.

sections.heading: The switch nobody talks about
sections.body:
People think switching grocers is about price. It's not. It's about the moment you realise you don't know where your food comes from — and you can't find out even if you try.

Ask your supermarket which farm raised your chicken. They can't tell you. Ask us, and we'll show you. That's our tagline for a reason: we'll show you where your food comes from.

We only stock food we'd feed our own families. That's not a slogan — it's the filter every product passes through. If it wouldn't go on Rachel's table, it doesn't go on the shelf.

sections.heading: Be part of what we're building
sections.body:
We're opening FarmThru up so you can own a piece of it. Not as a customer — as a partner.

We're preparing an equity crowdfunding campaign. If you believe the grocery system is broken and want to vote for change with your wallet, join the waitlist now.

Put down a $5 refundable deposit to reserve your spot. No commitment beyond that. You'll be first to know when the campaign goes live.

This is early. There are real risks — like any business changing how an industry works. But if you've already made the switch in your kitchen, this is a chance to back it with more than your grocery order.

sections.heading: Compliance
sections.body: FarmThru is preparing an equity crowdfunding campaign. Any investment carries risk, including the potential loss of your entire investment. Past performance is not a reliable indicator of future results. No returns are projected or guaranteed. This page is not financial advice. Please read the offer document carefully when available and consider whether this investment is appropriate for your circumstances.
